action.reasoning: Observation: Current FP-MLP baseline achieves avg_precision around 0.162-0.163 with val AP improving to 0.1708 over 10 epochs using pos_weights, AdamW, cosine annealing. Caches speed up loading. However, test AP slightly regressed, training loss has spikes, metrics not logged per-step/epoch to jsonl (curves uninformative), and model may benefit from better LR scheduling. Analysis: Pos weights help with imbalance (~1.4% positives), but cosine LR reaches 0 too quick; ReduceLROnPlateau on val_AP will adapt better for longer training. Proper per-epoch metric logging will enable curve tracking. Since time allows (~1min/epoch), increase to 25 epochs for further improvement without changing architecture (avoids load issues). Fix any loss masking to average only over labeled entries. Decision: Update train.py with complete code including proper masked BCE loss, val AP computation using sklearn per-task, ReduceLROnPlateau scheduler, metrics.jsonl logging each epoch, best model saving based on val AP. Keep model.py and predict.py unchanged for checkpoint compatibility. Train for 25 epochs then submit_eval on best checkpoint to get improved score.
